programmes.show_description: 1/8. Jason resigns himself to Tyler being separated from his sister, so he decides to give the kids a memorable time. Vic, however, is not one to give up without a fight.
2/8. Jason's landlord has decided to sell the flat. Nikki has a rude awakening at work. Contains language which some may find offensive.

Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.
7/15. A sister pleads for help to find the people who shot her brother.
Martin is on the Jurassic Coast in Devon, but it's vampires, rather than dinosaurs, that he's dealing with, thanks to the interior design tastes of the new owners of a one-bedroom flat.
Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams shop for antiques in the market town of Builth Wells, with experts Ben Cooper and Kate Bliss on hand to help them sniff out the best bargains.

programmes.show_name: BBC News at One including BBC Newsline
programmes.show_description: The latest national and international news from the BBC.
Mum Rachel needs Bear's help to repair her fractured bond with daughter Venice. Emotions run high, and tempers flare - can they learn to work as a team and build a better future?
2/8. Amanda and Alan tackle their first task, designing a Spanish dining room and foyer. Alan's dreams of a lavish reception area come crashing down, as he's confined to under the stairs.
34/50. Retired Jenny from Kent heads for the North Wessex Downs to be near her son, and with Jules Hudson's help, she's hoping to find a rosy cottage to call home.

programmes.show_name: The Repair Shop on the Road
programmes.show_description: A neglected phone box becomes a beacon of hope as Will and Liam turn it into a life-saving defibrillator station, and Dom uncovers a pair of secateurs owned by a D-Day paratrooper.
2/25. Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where knowledge equals speed. Ben, Habiba, Howie, Mia and Dave race in moving podiums across the studio to try to win £5,000.

programmes.show_description: 1/6. This week, Stacey and the team are tackling the Kings' home, where retired architectural technician Gerry and care worker Trish live with two of their three grown-up kids.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got News for You
programmes.show_description: 1/10. Alexander Armstrong is host, with guest panellists broadcaster Steph McGovern and comedian Phil Wang joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop. Contains adult humour.

Charlie Ross and the teams try to uncover profitable antiques in Sussex with experts Caroline Hawley and Tim Weeks. Charlie also learns about the science of thermoluminescence.

Bisexual mum-of-three Sophie has brought a third person into her marriage to husband Ricki. Now she is scared she will be pushed out. Can they find a way to make this throuple work?
3/8. Amanda and Alan brace themselves to tackle the heart of the B&B - the kitchen. Big plans are afoot with a Spanish-tiled extravaganza, but neither of them can agree on a colour scheme.
22/55. A career change means today's buyers urgently need Alistair Appleton's help in finding them a rural property in Derbyshire or Staffordshire.
